title: PilotFish Control Plane Recovery TSG
description: Troubleshooting guide for recovering PilotFish control plane during outages
key_dependencies: Identity-Service must be healthy before any PilotFish service starts
Config-Service provides runtime configuration; must be available at startup
Storage-Service data integrity must be verified before core restart
Monitoring-Service should be started last to avoid false alerts during recovery
critical_warnings: Never restart PilotFish-Core before Identity-Service and Config-Service are healthy
Always take a snapshot before attempting storage recovery
Verify quorum on distributed storage before restarting dependent services
Do not restart Scheduler until Core API is fully healthy to avoid duplicate job execution

sections.title: Pre-Recovery Checklist
content: Acknowledge the incident in the on-call system
Notify stakeholders via incident bridge
Check last known good configuration version in Config-Service
Verify network connectivity between all service nodes
Check disk space on all storage nodes (minimum 20% free required)
Confirm no active maintenance windows conflict with recovery
Pull latest TSG version from runbook store

sections.title: Service Startup Order
content: Step 1: Identity-Service - validate with GET /health, wait for 200
Step 2: Config-Service - validate with GET /health, confirm config version matches expected
Step 3: Storage-Service - run integrity check, validate with GET /health
Step 4: PilotFish-Core - start with --recovery-mode flag if data inconsistency suspected
Step 5: PilotFish-API - validate with smoke tests against /api/v1/status
Step 6: PilotFish-Scheduler - confirm no duplicate jobs created on startup
Step 7: PilotFish-Agent - agents will auto-reconnect, monitor connection count
Step 8: Monitoring-Service - restore alerting last to reduce noise during recovery

sections.title: Health Verification
content: All services expose /health endpoint returning {status: ok, version: x.y.z}
PilotFish-Core: check /api/v1/status for control plane state
PilotFish-Scheduler: verify job queue depth is non-zero and decreasing
PilotFish-Agent: monitor agent checkin rate in Monitoring-Service dashboard
Storage-Service: run consistency check command: storage-cli verify --full
End-to-end validation: submit test job and confirm completion within 5 minutes

sections.title: Rollback Procedures
content: If recovery fails after 45 minutes, initiate rollback to last known good state
Use snapshot taken during pre-recovery checklist
Rollback command: pilotfish-ctl rollback --to-snapshot <snapshot-id>
After rollback, verify all services with health checks before declaring success
Escalate to Tier 3 if rollback also fails
Document all actions taken in the incident ticket
